description: Bridge between user contact details (email, phone) and messaging identities via external identity servers. Enables invitations before account creation and contact binding.

actors.name: User
actors.description: Account owner binding or looking up an identity

actors.name: Homeserver
actors.description: Server proxying identity requests and enforcing rate limits

actors.name: Identity Server
actors.description: External service storing bindings between contact details and account identifiers

fields.name: address
fields.label: The email address or phone number being looked up or bound

fields.name: user_id
fields.label: The messaging account identifier to bind to the address

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: Client-generated secret that links validation steps in a session

fields.name: session_id
fields.label: Server-assigned identifier for the validation session

fields.name: id_server
fields.label: Domain of the identity server handling this request

fields.name: validated_at
fields.label: Timestamp when the identity server confirmed the address ownership

access: Identity server addresses must use the HTTPS scheme; HTTP is not permitted
Validation token requests are rate-limited per IP address and per third-party address
The client_secret must be present in all requests that continue a validation session
Third-party lookup can be disabled server-wide via configuration
Binding a third-party identifier to an account requires a successfully validated session
validation: Validation sessions have a time limit enforced by the identity server
Identity server responses must include the medium field to be considered successful

given: third-party lookup is enabled on the server
request is not rate-limited by IP or address
validation_token_sent.result: Validation message sent to the address; user must confirm receipt

given: user has confirmed the validation token
session_id and client_secret match the pending session

then.description: Validated third-party identifier recorded with timestamp

validation_completed.result: Identity server confirms ownership; address may now be bound to an account

then.description: Binding between address and account identifier stored on identity server

address_bound.result: Other users can invite this user by their email or phone number

given: lookup is enabled on the server
address_lookup.result: User ID associated with the address returned to requester

any: IP address has exceeded validation token request rate
address has exceeded validation token request rate

rate_limited.result: Request rejected until rate limit window resets

given: identity server does not respond within the timeout period
identity_server_unreachable.result: Operation fails; user should retry later

given: third-party lookup is disabled in server configuration

errors.message: Too many identity requests. Please wait before trying again.
Could not contact the identity server. Please try again later.
Third-party identifier lookup is not enabled on this server
Identity server address is invalid or uses an unsupported scheme
The validation session could not be found or the client secret is incorrect

events.name: identity.validation_requested
events.description: A validation token was requested for a third-party address

events.name: identity.validated
events.description: The user confirmed ownership of the third-party address

events.name: identity.bound
events.description: A third-party address was bound to an account identifier

events.name: identity.looked_up
events.description: A third-party address was resolved to an account identifier

related.reason: Third-party invites use identity lookup to invite users by email or phone
Email pushers verify their pushkey against the user's bound identities
